ACL.summary: A container for Access List Control commands.
ACL.args: CAT | DELUSER | DRYRUN | GENPASS | GETUSER | HELP | LIST | LOAD | LOG | SAVE | SETUSER | USERS | WHOAMI

ACL CAT.summary: Lists the ACL categories, or the commands inside a category.

ACL DELUSER.summary: Deletes ACL users, and terminates their connections.

ACL DRYRUN.summary: Simulates the execution of a command by a user, without executing the command.

ACL GENPASS.summary: Generates a pseudorandom, secure password that can be used to identify ACL users.

ACL GETUSER.summary: Lists the ACL rules of a user.

ACL HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

ACL LIST.summary: Dumps the effective rules in ACL file format.

ACL LOAD.summary: Reloads the rules from the configured ACL file.

ACL LOG.summary: Lists recent security events generated due to ACL rules.

ACL SAVE.summary: Saves the effective ACL rules in the configured ACL file.

ACL SETUSER.summary: Creates and modifies an ACL user and its rules.

ACL WHOAMI.summary: Returns the authenticated username of the current connection.

APPEND.summary: Appends a string to the value of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

ARCOUNT.summary: Returns the number of non-empty elements in an array.

ARDEL.summary: Deletes elements at the specified indices in an array.

ARDELRANGE.summary: Deletes elements in one or more ranges.

ARGET.summary: Gets the value at an index in an array.

ARGETRANGE.summary: Gets values in a range of indices.

ARGREP.args: key start end EXACT string | MATCH string | GLOB pattern | RE pattern [EXACT string | MATCH string | GLOB pattern | RE pattern ...] [AND | OR | LIMIT limit | WITHVALUES | NOCASE [AND | OR | LIMIT limit | WITHVALUES | NOCASE ...]]
ARGREP.summary: Searches array elements in a range using textual predicates.

ARINSERT.summary: Inserts one or more values at consecutive indices.

ARLASTITEMS.summary: Returns the most recently inserted elements.

ARLEN.summary: Returns the length of an array (max index + 1).

ARMGET.summary: Gets values at multiple indices in an array.

ARMSET.summary: Sets multiple index-value pairs in an array.

ARNEXT.summary: Returns the next index ARINSERT would use.

AROP.args: key start end SUM | MIN | MAX | AND | OR | XOR | MATCH value | USED
AROP.summary: Performs aggregate operations on array elements in a range.

ARRING.summary: Inserts values into a ring buffer of specified size, wrapping and truncating as needed.

ARSCAN.summary: Iterates existing elements in a range, returning index-value pairs.

ARSEEK.summary: Sets the ARINSERT / ARRING cursor to a specific index.

ARSET.summary: Sets one or more contiguous values starting at an index in an array.

ASKING.summary: Signals that a cluster client is following an -ASK redirect.

BF.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Bloom Filter

BF.CARD.summary: Returns the cardinality of a Bloom filter

BF.EXISTS.summary: Checks whether an item exists in a Bloom Filter

BF.INFO.summary: Returns information about a Bloom Filter

BF.INSERT.summary: Adds one or more items to a Bloom Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist

BF.LOADCHUNK.summary: Restores a filter previously saved using SCANDUMP

BF.MADD.summary: Adds one or more items to a Bloom Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist

BF.MEXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Bloom Filter

BF.SCANDUMP.summary: Begins an incremental save of the bloom filter

BGREWRITEAOF.summary: Asynchronously rewrites the append-only file to disk.

BGSAVE.summary: Asynchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

BITCOUNT.summary: Counts the number of set bits (population counting) in a string.

BITFIELD.args: key [encoding offset | [WRAP | SAT | FAIL] encoding offset value | encoding offset increment [encoding offset | [WRAP | SAT | FAIL] encoding offset value | encoding offset increment ...]]
BITFIELD.summary: Performs arbitrary bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITFIELD_RO.summary: Performs arbitrary read-only bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITOP.args: AND | OR | XOR | NOT | DIFF | DIFF1 | ANDOR | ONE destkey key [key ...]
BITOP.summary: Performs bitwise operations on multiple strings, and stores the result.

BITPOS.summary: Finds the first set (1) or clear (0) bit in a string.

BLMOVE.args: source destination LEFT | RIGHT LEFT | RIGHT timeout
BLMOVE.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

BLMPOP.args: timeout numkeys key [key ...] LEFT | RIGHT [COUNT count]
BLMPOP.summary: Pops the first element from one of multiple lists. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BLPOP.summary: Removes and returns the first element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BRPOP.summary: Removes and returns the last element in a list. Blocks until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BRPOPLPUSH.summary: Pops an element from a list, pushes it to another list and returns it. Block until an element is available otherwise. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

BZMPOP.args: timeout numkeys key [key ...] MIN | MAX [COUNT count]
BZMPOP.summary: Removes and returns a member by score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

BZPOPMAX.summary: Removes and returns the member with the highest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member available otherwise.  Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

BZPOPMIN.summary: Removes and returns the member with the lowest score from one or more sorted sets. Blocks until a member is available otherwise. Deletes the sorted set if the last element was popped.

CF.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Cuckoo Filter

CF.ADDNX.summary: Adds an item to a Cuckoo Filter if the item did not exist previously.

CF.COUNT.summary: Return the number of times an item might be in a Cuckoo Filter

CF.DEL.summary: Deletes an item from a Cuckoo Filter

CF.EXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Cuckoo Filter

CF.INFO.summary: Returns information about a Cuckoo Filter

CF.INSERT.summary: Adds one or more items to a Cuckoo Filter. A filter will be created if it does not exist

CF.INSERTNX.summary: Adds one or more items to a Cuckoo Filter if the items did not exist previously. A filter will be created if it does not exist

CF.LOADCHUNK.summary: Restores a filter previously saved using SCANDUMP

CF.MEXISTS.summary: Checks whether one or more items exist in a Cuckoo Filter

CF.SCANDUMP.summary: Begins an incremental save of the bloom filter

CLIENT.summary: A container for client connection commands.
CLIENT.args: CACHING | GETNAME | GETREDIR | HELP | ID | INFO | KILL | LIST | NO-EVICT | NO-TOUCH | PAUSE | REPLY | SETINFO | SETNAME | TRACKING | TRACKINGINFO | UNBLOCK | UNPAUSE

CLIENT CACHING.summary: Instructs the server whether to track the keys in the next request.

CLIENT GETNAME.summary: Returns the name of the connection.

CLIENT GETREDIR.summary: Returns the client ID to which the connection's tracking notifications are redirected.

CLIENT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CLIENT ID.summary: Returns the unique client ID of the connection.

CLIENT KILL.args: ip:port | [ID client-id] | [NORMAL | MASTER | SLAVE | REPLICA | PUBSUB] | [USER username] | [ADDR ip:port] | [LADDR ip:port] | [YES | NO] | [MAXAGE maxage] [[ID client-id] | [NORMAL | MASTER | SLAVE | REPLICA | PUBSUB] | [USER username] | [ADDR ip:port] | [LADDR ip:port] | [YES | NO] | [MAXAGE maxage] ...]

CLIENT NO-EVICT.summary: Sets the client eviction mode of the connection.

CLIENT NO-TOUCH.summary: Controls whether commands sent by the client affect the LRU/LFU of accessed keys.

CLIENT REPLY.summary: Instructs the server whether to reply to commands.

CLIENT SETINFO.summary: Sets information specific to the client or connection.

CLIENT TRACKING.summary: Controls server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.

CLIENT TRACKINGINFO.summary: Returns information about server-assisted client-side caching for the connection.

CLIENT UNBLOCK.summary: Unblocks a client blocked by a blocking command from a different connection.

CLIENT UNPAUSE.summary: Resumes processing commands from paused clients.

CLUSTER.summary: A container for Redis Cluster commands.
CLUSTER.args: ADDSLOTS | ADDSLOTSRANGE | BUMPEPOCH | COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS | COUNTKEYSINSLOT | DELSLOTS | DELSLOTSRANGE | FAILOVER | FLUSHSLOTS | FORGET | GETKEYSINSLOT | HELP | INFO | KEYSLOT | LINKS | MEET | MIGRATION | MYID | MYSHARDID | NODES | REPLICAS | REPLICATE | RESET | SAVECONFIG | SET-CONFIG-EPOCH | SETSLOT | SHARDS | SLAVES | SLOT-STATS | SLOTS | SYNCSLOTS

CLUSTER ADDSLOTS.summary: Assigns new hash slots to a node.

CLUSTER ADDSLOTSRANGE.summary: Assigns new hash slot ranges to a node.

CLUSTER COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS.summary: Returns the number of active failure reports active for a node.

CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT.summary: Returns the number of keys in a hash slot.

CLUSTER DELSLOTS.summary: Sets hash slots as unbound for a node.

CLUSTER DELSLOTSRANGE.summary: Sets hash slot ranges as unbound for a node.

CLUSTER FAILOVER.summary: Forces a replica to perform a manual failover of its master.

CLUSTER FLUSHSLOTS.summary: Deletes all slots information from a node.

CLUSTER FORGET.summary: Removes a node from the nodes table.

CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT.summary: Returns the key names in a hash slot.

CLUSTER HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CLUSTER INFO.summary: Returns information about the state of a node.

CLUSTER KEYSLOT.summary: Returns the hash slot for a key.

CLUSTER LINKS.summary: Returns a list of all TCP links to and from peer nodes.

CLUSTER MEET.summary: Forces a node to handshake with another node.

CLUSTER MIGRATION.args: start-slot end-slot [start-slot end-slot ...] | ID task-id | ALL | [ID task-id] | [ALL]
CLUSTER MIGRATION.summary: Start, monitor and cancel slot migration.

CLUSTER MYID.summary: Returns the ID of a node.

CLUSTER MYSHARDID.summary: Returns the shard ID of a node.

CLUSTER NODES.summary: Returns the cluster configuration for a node.

CLUSTER REPLICAS.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.

CLUSTER REPLICATE.summary: Configure a node as replica of a master node.

CLUSTER SAVECONFIG.summary: Forces a node to save the cluster configuration to disk.

CLUSTER SET-CONFIG-EPOCH.summary: Sets the configuration epoch for a new node.

CLUSTER SETSLOT.args: slot IMPORTING node-id | MIGRATING node-id | NODE node-id | STABLE
CLUSTER SETSLOT.summary: Binds a hash slot to a node.

CLUSTER SHARDS.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to shards.

CLUSTER SLAVES.summary: Lists the replica nodes of a master node.

CLUSTER SLOT-STATS.summary: Return an array of slot usage statistics for slots assigned to the current node.

CLUSTER SLOTS.summary: Returns the mapping of cluster slots to nodes.

CLUSTER SYNCSLOTS.args: task-id start-slot end-slot [start-slot end-slot ...] | RDBCHANNEL task-id | SNAPSHOT-EOF | STREAM-EOF | state offset | FAIL error | option [option ...] value [value ...]
CLUSTER SYNCSLOTS.summary: Internal command for atomic slot migration protocol between cluster nodes.

CMS.INCRBY.summary: Increases the count of one or more items by increment

CMS.INITBYDIM.summary: Initializes a Count-Min Sketch to dimensions specified by user

CMS.INITBYPROB.summary: Initializes a Count-Min Sketch to accommodate requested tolerances.

CMS.MERGE.summary: Merges several sketches into one sketch

CMS.QUERY.summary: Returns the count for one or more items in a sketch

COMMAND.summary: Returns detailed information about all commands.
COMMAND.args: COUNT | DOCS | GETKEYS | GETKEYSANDFLAGS | HELP | INFO | LIST

COMMAND DOCS.summary: Returns documentary information about one, multiple or all commands.

COMMAND GETKEYS.summary: Extracts the key names from an arbitrary command.

COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS.summary: Extracts the key names and access flags for an arbitrary command.

COMMAND HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

COMMAND INFO.summary: Returns information about one, multiple or all commands.

COMMAND LIST.summary: Returns a list of command names.

CONFIG.summary: A container for server configuration commands.

CONFIG GET.summary: Returns the effective values of configuration parameters.

CONFIG HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

CONFIG REWRITE.summary: Persists the effective configuration to file.

COPY.summary: Copies the value of a key to a new key.

DBSIZE.summary: Returns the number of keys in the database.

DECR.summary: Decrements the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DECRBY.summary: Decrements a number from the integer value of a key. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

DELEX.args: key [IFEQ ifeq-value | IFNE ifne-value | IFDEQ ifdeq-digest | IFDNE ifdne-digest]
DELEX.summary: Conditionally removes the specified key based on value or digest comparison.

DIGEST.summary: Returns the XXH3 hash of a string value.

DUMP.summary: Returns a serialized representation of the value stored at a key.

EVALSHA.summary: Executes a server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

EVALSHA_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script by SHA1 digest.

EVAL_RO.summary: Executes a read-only server-side Lua script.

EXEC.summary: Executes all commands in a transaction.

EXISTS.summary: Determines whether one or more keys exist.

EXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in seconds.

EXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix timestamp.

EXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix timestamp.

FAILOVER.summary: Starts a coordinated failover from a server to one of its replicas.

FLUSHALL.summary: Removes all keys from all databases.

FLUSHDB.summary: Remove all keys from the current database.

FT.AGGREGATE.args: index query [VERBATIM] [LOAD count field [field ...]] [TIMEOUT timeout] [LOAD *] [GROUPBY nargs property [property ...] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] ...]] [GROUPBY nargs property [property ...] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] ...]] ...]] [SORTBY nargs [property ASC | DESC [property ASC | DESC ...]] [MAX num]] [                             AS name [                             AS name ...]] [LIMIT offset num] [FILTER filter] [WITHCURSOR [COUNT read_size] [MAXIDLE idle_time]] [PARAMS nargs name value [name value ...]] [DIALECT dialect]
FT.AGGREGATE.summary: Run a search query on an index and perform aggregate transformations on the results

FT.ALIASADD.summary: Adds an alias to the index

FT.ALIASDEL.summary: Deletes an alias from the index

FT.ALIASUPDATE.summary: Adds or updates an alias to the index

FT.ALTER.summary: Adds a new field to the index

FT.CONFIG HELP.summary: Help description of runtime configuration options

FT.CREATE.args: index [HASH | JSON] [ENABLE | DISABLE] [PREFIX count prefix [prefix ...]] [FILTER filter] [LANGUAGE default_lang] [LANGUAGE_FIELD lang_attribute] [SCORE default_score] [SCORE_FIELD score_attribute] [PAYLOAD_FIELD payload_attribute] [MAXTEXTFIELDS] [TEMPORARY seconds] [NOOFFSETS] [NOHL] [NOFIELDS] [NOFREQS] [count [stopword [stopword ...]]] [SKIPINITIALSCAN] SCHEMA field_name [AS alias] TEXT | TAG | NUMERIC | GEO | GEOSHAPE | VECTOR [WITHSUFFIXTRIE] [INDEXEMPTY] [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] [field_name [AS alias] TEXT | TAG | NUMERIC | GEO | GEOSHAPE | VECTOR [WITHSUFFIXTRIE] [INDEXEMPTY] [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] ...]
FT.CREATE.summary: Creates an index with the given spec

FT.DICTDUMP.summary: Dumps all terms in the given dictionary

FT.EXPLAIN.summary: Returns the execution plan for a complex query

FT.EXPLAINCLI.summary: Returns the execution plan for a complex query

FT.HYBRID.args: index SEARCH query [SCORER scorer] [YIELD_SCORE_AS yield_score_as] VSIM field vector [KNN count K k [EF_RUNTIME ef_runtime] [SHARD_K_RATIO shard_k_ratio] [YIELD_SCORE_AS yield_score_as] | RANGE count RADIUS radius [EPSILON epsilon] [YIELD_SCORE_AS yield_score_as]] [FILTER filter] [COMBINE RRF count [CONSTANT constant] [WINDOW window] [YIELD_SCORE_AS yield_score_as] | LINEAR count [ALPHA alpha BETA beta] [WINDOW window] [YIELD_SCORE_AS yield_score_as]] [LIMIT offset num] [SORTBY sortby [ASC | DESC] | NOSORT] [PARAMS nargs name value [name value ...]] [TIMEOUT timeout] [FORMAT format] [LOAD count field [field ...]] [LOAD *] [GROUPBY nproperties property [property ...] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] [REDUCE COUNT | COUNT_DISTINCT | COUNT_DISTINCTISH | SUM | MIN | MAX | AVG | STDDEV | QUANTILE | TOLIST | FIRST_VALUE | RANDOM_SAMPLE nargs arg [arg ...] [AS name] | REDUCE COLLECT nargs FIELDS num_fields field [field ...] | FIELDS * [SORTBY nargs field [ASC | DESC] [field [ASC | DESC] ...]] [LIMIT offset count] [AS name] ...]]] [                             AS name [                             AS name ...]] [count filter_expression [ADHOC | BATCHES] [BATCH_SIZE batch_size_value]]
FT.HYBRID.summary: Performs hybrid search combining text search and vector similarity search

FT.INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics on the index

FT.PROFILE.args: index SEARCH | AGGREGATE | HYBRID [LIMITED] QUERY query
FT.PROFILE.summary: Performs a `FT.SEARCH`, `FT.AGGREGATE`, or `FT.HYBRID` command and collects performance information

FT.SEARCH.args: index query [NOCONTENT] [VERBATIM] [NOSTOPWORDS] [WITHSCORES] [WITHPAYLOADS] [WITHSORTKEYS] [FILTER numeric_field min max [FILTER numeric_field min max ...]] [GEOFILTER geo_field lon lat radius m | km | mi | ft [GEOFILTER geo_field lon lat radius m | km | mi | ft ...]] [INKEYS count key [key ...]] [INFIELDS count field [field ...]] [RETURN count identifier [AS property] [identifier [AS property] ...]] [SUMMARIZE [FIELDS count field [field ...]] [FRAGS num] [LEN fragsize] [SEPARATOR separator]] [HIGHLIGHT [FIELDS count field [field ...]] [TAGS open close]] [SLOP slop] [TIMEOUT timeout] [INORDER] [LANGUAGE language] [EXPANDER expander] [SCORER scorer] [EXPLAINSCORE] [PAYLOAD payload] [SORTBY sortby [ASC | DESC]] [LIMIT offset num] [PARAMS nargs name value [name value ...]] [DIALECT dialect]
FT.SEARCH.summary: Searches the index with a textual query, returning either documents or just ids

FT.SPELLCHECK.args: index query [DISTANCE distance] [INCLUDE | EXCLUDE dictionary [terms [terms ...]]] [DIALECT dialect]
FT.SPELLCHECK.summary: Performs spelling correction on a query, returning suggestions for misspelled terms

FT.SUGADD.summary: Adds a suggestion string to an auto-complete suggestion dictionary

FT.SUGDEL.summary: Deletes a string from a suggestion index

FT.SUGGET.summary: Gets completion suggestions for a prefix

FT.SUGLEN.summary: Gets the size of an auto-complete suggestion dictionary

FT.SYNDUMP.summary: Dumps the contents of a synonym group

FT.SYNUPDATE.summary: Creates or updates a synonym group with additional terms

FT.TAGVALS.summary: Returns the distinct tags indexed in a Tag field

FT._ALIASADDIFNX.summary: Adds an alias to the index

FT._ALIASDELIFX.summary: Deletes an alias from the index

FT._ALTERIFNX.summary: Adds a new field to the index

FT._CREATEIFNX.args: index [HASH | JSON] [ENABLE | DISABLE] [PREFIX count prefix [prefix ...]] [FILTER filter] [LANGUAGE default_lang] [LANGUAGE_FIELD lang_attribute] [SCORE default_score] [SCORE_FIELD score_attribute] [PAYLOAD_FIELD payload_attribute] [MAXTEXTFIELDS] [TEMPORARY seconds] [NOOFFSETS] [NOHL] [NOFIELDS] [NOFREQS] [count [stopword [stopword ...]]] [SKIPINITIALSCAN] SCHEMA field_name [AS alias] TEXT | TAG | NUMERIC | GEO | GEOSHAPE | VECTOR [WITHSUFFIXTRIE] [INDEXEMPTY] [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] [field_name [AS alias] TEXT | TAG | NUMERIC | GEO | GEOSHAPE | VECTOR [WITHSUFFIXTRIE] [INDEXEMPTY] [INDEXMISSING] [SORTABLE [UNF]] [NOINDEX] ...]
FT._CREATEIFNX.summary: Creates an index with the given spec

FT._LIST.summary: Returns a list of all existing indexes

FUNCTION.args: DELETE | DUMP | FLUSH | HELP | KILL | LIST | LOAD | RESTORE | STATS

FUNCTION DELETE.summary: Deletes a library and its functions.

FUNCTION DUMP.summary: Dumps all libraries into a serialized binary payload.

FUNCTION HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

FUNCTION RESTORE.summary: Restores all libraries from a payload.

FUNCTION STATS.summary: Returns information about a function during execution.

GEOADD.args: key [NX | XX] [CH] longitude latitude member [longitude latitude member ...]
GEOADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a geospatial index. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

GEODIST.args: key member1 member2 [M | KM | FT | MI]
GEODIST.summary: Returns the distance between two members of a geospatial index.

GEOHASH.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index as geohash strings.

GEOPOS.summary: Returns the longitude and latitude of members from a geospatial index.

GEORADIUS.args: key longitude latitude radius M | KM | FT | MI [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH] [COUNT count [ANY]] [ASC | DESC] [STORE key | STOREDIST key]
GEORADIUS.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a coordinate, optionally stores the result.

GEORADIUSBYMEMBER.args: key member radius M | KM | FT | MI [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH] [COUNT count [ANY]] [ASC | DESC] [STORE key | STOREDIST key]
GEORADIUSBYMEMBER.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members within a distance from a member, optionally stores the result.

GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO.args: key member radius M | KM | FT | MI [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH] [COUNT count [ANY]] [ASC | DESC]
GEORADIUSBYMEMBER_RO.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a member.

GEORADIUS_RO.args: key longitude latitude radius M | KM | FT | MI [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH] [COUNT count [ANY]] [ASC | DESC]
GEORADIUS_RO.summary: Returns members from a geospatial index that are within a distance from a coordinate.

GEOSEARCH.args: key FROMMEMBER member | longitude latitude BYRADIUS radius M | KM | FT | MI | BYBOX width height M | KM | FT | MI [ASC | DESC] [COUNT count [ANY]] [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH]
GEOSEARCH.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle.

GEOSEARCHSTORE.args: destination source FROMMEMBER member | longitude latitude BYRADIUS radius M | KM | FT | MI | BYBOX width height M | KM | FT | MI [ASC | DESC] [COUNT count [ANY]] [STOREDIST]
GEOSEARCHSTORE.summary: Queries a geospatial index for members inside an area of a box or a circle, optionally stores the result.

GET.summary: Returns the string value of a key.

GETBIT.summary: Returns a bit value by offset.

GETDEL.summary: Returns the string value of a key after deleting the key.

GETEX.args: key [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | PERSIST]
GETEX.summary: Returns the string value of a key after setting its expiration time.

GETRANGE.summary: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.

GETSET.summary: Returns the previous string value of a key after setting it to a new value.

HDEL.summary: Deletes one or more fields and their values from a hash. Deletes the hash if no fields remain.

HEXISTS.summary: Determines whether a field exists in a hash.

HEXPIRE.args: key seconds [NX | XX | GT | LT] numfields field [field ...]
HEXPIRE.summary: Set expiry for hash field using relative time to expire (seconds)

HEXPIREAT.args: key unix-time-seconds [NX | XX | GT | LT] numfields field [field ...]
HEXPIREAT.summary: Set expiry for hash field using an absolute Unix timestamp (seconds)

HEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in seconds.

HGET.summary: Returns the value of a field in a hash.

HGETALL.summary: Returns all fields and values in a hash.

HGETDEL.summary: Returns the value of a field and deletes it from the hash.

HGETEX.args: key [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | PERSIST] numfields field [field ...]
HGETEX.summary: Get the value of one or more fields of a given hash key, and optionally set their expiration.

HINCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a field in a hash by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HINCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increments the floating point value of a field by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the field doesn't exist.

HKEYS.summary: Returns all fields in a hash.

HLEN.summary: Returns the number of fields in a hash.

HMGET.summary: Returns the values of all fields in a hash.

HMSET.summary: Sets the values of multiple fields.

HOTKEYS.summary: A container for hotkeys tracking commands.

HOTKEYS GET.summary: Returns lists of top K hotkeys depending on metrics chosen in HOTKEYS START command.

HOTKEYS HELP.summary: Return helpful text about HOTKEYS command parameters.

HOTKEYS RESET.summary: Release the resources used for hotkey tracking.

HPERSIST.summary: Removes the expiration time for each specified field

HPEXPIRE.args: key milliseconds [NX | XX | GT | LT] numfields field [field ...]
HPEXPIRE.summary: Set expiry for hash field using relative time to expire (milliseconds)

HPEXPIREAT.args: key unix-time-milliseconds [NX | XX | GT | LT] numfields field [field ...]
HPEXPIREAT.summary: Set expiry for hash field using an absolute Unix timestamp (milliseconds)

HPEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a hash field as a Unix timestamp, in msec.

HPTTL.summary: Returns the TTL in milliseconds of a hash field.

HRANDFIELD.summary: Returns one or more random fields from a hash.

HSCAN.summary: Iterates over fields and values of a hash.

HSET.summary: Creates or modifies the value of a field in a hash.

HSETEX.args: key [FNX | FXX] [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL] numfields field value [field value ...]
HSETEX.summary: Set the value of one or more fields of a given hash key, and optionally set their expiration.

HSETNX.summary: Sets the value of a field in a hash only when the field doesn't exist.

HSTRLEN.summary: Returns the length of the value of a field.

HTTL.summary: Returns the TTL in seconds of a hash field.

HVALS.summary: Returns all values in a hash.

INCR.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by one. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBY.summary: Increments the integer value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCRBYFLOAT.summary: Increment the floating point value of a key by a number. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INCREX.args: key [BYFLOAT float | BYINT integer] [SATURATE] [LBOUND lowerbound] [UBOUND upperbound] [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | PERSIST] [ENX]
INCREX.summary: Increments the numeric value of a key by a number and sets its expiration time. Uses 0 as initial value if the key doesn't exist.

INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics about the server.

JSON.ARRAPPEND.summary: Append the JSON values into the array at path after the last element in it

JSON.ARRINDEX.summary: Search for the first occurrence of a JSON value in an array

JSON.ARRINSERT.summary: Insert the json values into the array at path before the index (shifts to the right)

JSON.ARRLEN.summary: Report the length of the JSON array at path in key

JSON.ARRPOP.summary: Remove and return the element at the specified index in the array at path

JSON.ARRTRIM.summary: Trim an array so that it contains only the specified inclusive range of elements

JSON.CLEAR.summary: Clear container values (arrays/objects) and set numeric values to 0

JSON.DEBUG.summary: This is a container command for debugging related tasks

JSON.MERGE.summary: Merge a given JSON value into matching paths. Consequently, JSON values at matching paths are updated, deleted, or expanded with new children

JSON.MGET.summary: Return the values at path from multiple key arguments

JSON.MSET.summary: Set or update one or more JSON values according to the specified key-path-value triplets

JSON.NUMINCRBY.summary: Increment the number value stored at path by number

JSON.NUMMULTBY.summary: Multiply the number value stored at path by number

JSON.NUMPOWBY.summary: Raise the number value stored at path to the power of number

JSON.OBJKEYS.summary: Return the keys in the object that's referenced by path

JSON.OBJLEN.summary: Report the number of keys in the JSON object at path in key

JSON.RESP.summary: Return the JSON in key in Redis serialization protocol specification form

JSON.SET.args: key path json [NX | XX] [BF16 | FP16 | FP32 | FP64] [STRING | JSON | BSON]
JSON.SET.summary: Set the JSON value at path in key

JSON.STRAPPEND.summary: Append the json-string values to the string at path

JSON.STRLEN.summary: Report the length of the JSON String at path in key

JSON.TOGGLE.summary: Toggle the boolean value stored at path

JSON.TYPE.summary: Report the type of JSON value at path

KEYS.summary: Returns all key names that match a pattern.

LASTSAVE.summary: Returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful save to disk.

LATENCY.summary: A container for latency diagnostics commands.
LATENCY.args: DOCTOR | GRAPH | HELP | HISTOGRAM | HISTORY | LATEST | RESET

LATENCY DOCTOR.summary: Returns a human-readable latency analysis report.

LATENCY GRAPH.summary: Returns a latency graph for an event.

LATENCY HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

LATENCY HISTOGRAM.summary: Returns the cumulative distribution of latencies of a subset or all commands.

LATENCY HISTORY.summary: Returns timestamp-latency samples for an event.

LATENCY LATEST.summary: Returns the latest latency samples for all events.

LATENCY RESET.summary: Resets the latency data for one or more events.

LINDEX.summary: Returns an element from a list by its index.

LINSERT.summary: Inserts an element before or after another element in a list.

LLEN.summary: Returns the length of a list.

LMOVE.args: source destination LEFT | RIGHT LEFT | RIGHT
LMOVE.summary: Returns an element after popping it from one list and pushing it to another. Deletes the list if the last element was moved.

LMPOP.summary: Returns multiple elements from a list after removing them. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

LOLWUT.summary: Displays computer art and the Redis version

LPOP.summary: Returns the first elements in a list after removing it. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

LPOS.summary: Returns the index of matching elements in a list.

LPUSH.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

LPUSHX.summary: Prepends one or more elements to a list only when the list exists.

LRANGE.summary: Returns a range of elements from a list.

LREM.summary: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.

LSET.summary: Sets the value of an element in a list by its index.

LTRIM.summary: Removes elements from both ends a list. Deletes the list if all elements were trimmed.

MEMORY.summary: A container for memory diagnostics commands.
MEMORY.args: DOCTOR | HELP | MALLOC-STATS | PURGE | STATS | USAGE

MEMORY HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

MEMORY PURGE.summary: Asks the allocator to release memory.

MEMORY USAGE.summary: Estimates the memory usage of a key.

MGET.summary: Atomically returns the string values of one or more keys.

MIGRATE.args: host port key | empty-string destination-db timeout [COPY] [REPLACE] [AUTH password | username password] [KEYS key [KEYS key ...]]
MIGRATE.summary: Atomically transfers a key from one Redis instance to another.

MODULE HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

MODULE LOADEX.summary: Loads a module using extended parameters.

MONITOR.summary: Listens for all requests received by the server in real-time.

MOVE.summary: Moves a key to another database.

MSET.summary: Atomically creates or modifies the string values of one or more keys.

MSETEX.args: numkeys key value [key value ...] [NX | XX] [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
MSETEX.summary: Atomically sets multiple string keys with a shared expiration in a single operation. Supports flexible argument parsing where condition and expiration flags can appear in any order.

MSETNX.summary: Atomically modifies the string values of one or more keys only when all keys don't exist.

OBJECT.summary: A container for object introspection commands.

OBJECT ENCODING.summary: Returns the internal encoding of a Redis object.

OBJECT FREQ.summary: Returns the logarithmic access frequency counter of a Redis object.

OBJECT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

OBJECT IDLETIME.summary: Returns the time since the last access to a Redis object.

OBJECT REFCOUNT.summary: Returns the reference count of a value of a key.

PERSIST.summary: Removes the expiration time of a key.

PEXPIRE.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key in milliseconds.

PEXPIREAT.summary: Sets the expiration time of a key to a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PEXPIRETIME.summary: Returns the expiration time of a key as a Unix milliseconds timestamp.

PFADD.summary: Adds elements to a HyperLogLog key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

PFCOUNT.summary: Returns the approximated cardinality of the set(s) observed by the HyperLogLog key(s).

PFDEBUG.summary: Internal commands for debugging HyperLogLog values.

PFMERGE.summary: Merges one or more HyperLogLog values into a single key.

PFSELFTEST.summary: An internal command for testing HyperLogLog values.

PSETEX.summary: Sets both string value and expiration time in milliseconds of a key. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

PSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

PSYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

PTTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in milliseconds of a key.

PUBLISH.summary: Posts a message to a channel.

PUBSUB.args: CHANNELS | HELP | NUMPAT | NUMSUB | SHARDCHANNELS | SHARDNUMSUB

PUBSUB HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

PUBSUB NUMPAT.summary: Returns a count of unique pattern subscriptions.

PUBSUB NUMSUB.summary: Returns a count of subscribers to channels.

PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB.summary: Returns the count of subscribers of shard channels.

PUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages published to channels that match one or more patterns.

RANDOMKEY.summary: Returns a random key name from the database.

READONLY.summary: Enables read-only queries for a connection to a Redis Cluster replica node.

READWRITE.summary: Enables read-write queries for a connection to a Reids Cluster replica node.

RENAME.summary: Renames a key and overwrites the destination.

RENAMENX.summary: Renames a key only when the target key name doesn't exist.

REPLCONF.summary: An internal command for configuring the replication stream.

REPLICAOF.summary: Configures a server as replica of another, or promotes it to a master.

RESTORE.summary: Creates a key from the serialized representation of a value.

RESTORE-ASKING.summary: An internal command for migrating keys in a cluster.

RPOP.summary: Returns and removes the last elements of a list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

RPOPLPUSH.summary: Returns the last element of a list after removing and pushing it to another list. Deletes the list if the last element was popped.

RPUSH.summary: Appends one or more elements to a list. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

RPUSHX.summary: Appends an element to a list only when the list exists.

SADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a set. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SAVE.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk.

SCAN.summary: Iterates over the key names in the database.

SCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a set.

SCRIPT.summary: A container for Lua scripts management commands.
SCRIPT.args: DEBUG | EXISTS | FLUSH | HELP | KILL | LOAD

SCRIPT DEBUG.summary: Sets the debug mode of server-side Lua scripts.

SCRIPT EXISTS.summary: Determines whether server-side Lua scripts exist in the script cache.

SCRIPT FLUSH.summary: Removes all server-side Lua scripts from the script cache.

SCRIPT HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

SCRIPT KILL.summary: Terminates a server-side Lua script during execution.

SCRIPT LOAD.summary: Loads a server-side Lua script to the script cache.

SDIFF.summary: Returns the difference of multiple sets.

SDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sets in a key.

SET.args: key value [NX | XX | IFEQ ifeq-value | IFNE ifne-value | IFDEQ ifdeq-digest | IFDNE ifdne-digest] [GET] [EX seconds | PX milliseconds | EXAT unix-time-seconds | PXAT unix-time-milliseconds | KEEPTTL]
SET.summary: Sets the string value of a key, ignoring its type. The key is created if it doesn't exist.

SETBIT.summary: Sets or clears the bit at offset of the string value. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETEX.summary: Sets the string value and expiration time of a key. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SETNX.summary: Set the string value of a key only when the key doesn't exist.

SETRANGE.summary: Overwrites a part of a string value with another by an offset. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

SHUTDOWN.summary: Synchronously saves the database(s) to disk and shuts down the Redis server.

SINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sets.

SINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sets.

SINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sets in a key.

SISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether a member belongs to a set.

SLAVEOF.summary: Sets a Redis server as a replica of another, or promotes it to being a master.

SLOWLOG.summary: A container for slow log commands.

SLOWLOG HELP.summary: Show helpful text about the different subcommands

SLOWLOG LEN.summary: Returns the number of entries in the slow log.

SLOWLOG RESET.summary: Clears all entries from the slow log.

SMEMBERS.summary: Returns all members of a set.

SMISMEMBER.summary: Determines whether multiple members belong to a set.

SMOVE.summary: Moves a member from one set to another.

SORT.args: key [BY pattern] [offset count] [GET pattern [GET pattern ...]] [ASC | DESC] [ALPHA] [STORE destination]
SORT.summary: Sorts the elements in a list, a set, or a sorted set, optionally storing the result.

SORT_RO.args: key [BY pattern] [offset count] [GET pattern [GET pattern ...]] [ASC | DESC] [ALPHA]
SORT_RO.summary: Returns the sorted elements of a list, a set, or a sorted set.

SPOP.summary: Returns one or more random members from a set after removing them. Deletes the set if the last member was popped.

SPUBLISH.summary: Post a message to a shard channel

SRANDMEMBER.summary: Get one or multiple random members from a set

SREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a set. Deletes the set if the last member was removed.

SSCAN.summary: Iterates over members of a set.

SSUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to shard channels.

STRLEN.summary: Returns the length of a string value.

SUBSCRIBE.summary: Listens for messages published to channels.

SUBSTR.summary: Returns a substring from a string value.

SUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sets.

SUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sets in a key.

SUNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to shard channels.

SYNC.summary: An internal command used in replication.

TDIGEST.ADD.summary: Adds one or more observations to a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.BYRANK.summary: Returns, for each input rank, an estimation of the value (floating-point) with that rank

TDIGEST.BYREVRANK.summary: Returns, for each input reverse rank, an estimation of the value (floating-point) with that reverse rank

TDIGEST.CDF.summary: Returns, for each input value, an estimation of the floating-point fraction of (observations smaller than the given value + half the observations equal to the given value). Multiple fractions can be retrieved in a single call.

TDIGEST.CREATE.summary: Allocates memory and initializes a new t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics about a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.MAX.summary: Returns the maximum observation value from a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.MERGE.summary: Merges multiple t-digest sketches into a single sketch

TDIGEST.MIN.summary: Returns the minimum observation value from a t-digest sketch

TDIGEST.QUANTILE.summary: Returns, for each input fraction, an estimation of the value (floating point) that is smaller than the given fraction of observations

TDIGEST.RANK.summary: Returns, for each input value (floating-point), the estimated rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are smaller than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)

TDIGEST.RESET.summary: Resets a t-digest sketch: empty the sketch and re-initializes it.

TDIGEST.REVRANK.summary: Returns, for each input value (floating-point), the estimated reverse rank of the value (the number of observations in the sketch that are larger than the value + half the number of observations that are equal to the value)

TDIGEST.TRIMMED_MEAN.summary: Returns an estimation of the mean value from the sketch, excluding observation values outside the low and high cutoff quantiles

TOPK.ADD.summary: Adds an item to a Top-k sketch. Multiple items can be added at the same time.

TOPK.COUNT.summary: Return the count for one or more items are in a sketch

TOPK.INCRBY.summary: Increases the count of one or more items by increment

TOPK.LIST.summary: Return the full list of items in Top-K sketch.

TOPK.QUERY.summary: Checks whether one or more items are in a sketch

TOPK.RESERVE.summary: Initializes a Top-K sketch with specified parameters

TOUCH.summary: Returns the number of existing keys out of those specified after updating the time they were last accessed.

TRIMSLOTS.summary: Trim the keys that belong to specified slots.

TS.ADD.args: key timestamp value [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [ENCODING COMPRESSED | UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [ON_DUPLICATE BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [IGNORE ignoreMaxTimediff ignoreMaxValDiff] [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
TS.ADD.summary: Append a sample to a time series

TS.ALTER.args: key [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [IGNORE ignoreMaxTimediff ignoreMaxValDiff] [LABELS [label value [label value ...]]]
TS.ALTER.summary: Update the retention, chunk size, duplicate policy, and labels of an existing time series

TS.CREATE.args: key [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [ENCODING COMPRESSED | UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [IGNORE ignoreMaxTimediff ignoreMaxValDiff] [LABELS [label value [label value ...]]]

TS.CREATERULE.args: sourceKey destKey AGGREGATION avg | sum | min | max | range | count | first | last | std.p | std.s | var.p | var.s | twa | countnan | countall bucketDuration [alignTimestamp]

TS.DECRBY.args: key subtrahend [TIMESTAMP timestamp] [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [ENCODING COMPRESSED | UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [IGNORE ignoreMaxTimeDiff ignoreMaxValDiff] [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
TS.DECRBY.summary: Decrease the value of the latest sample

TS.DEL.summary: Delete all samples between two timestamps for a given time series

TS.GET.summary: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from a given time series

TS.INCRBY.args: key addend [TIMESTAMP timestamp] [RETENTION retentionPeriod] [ENCODING COMPRESSED | UNCOMPRESSED] [CHUNK_SIZE size] [DUPLICATE_POLICY BLOCK | FIRST | LAST | MIN | MAX | SUM] [IGNORE ignoreMaxTimeDiff ignoreMaxValDiff] [LABELS label value [label value ...]]
TS.INCRBY.summary: Increase the value of the latest sample

TS.INFO.summary: Returns information and statistics for a time series

TS.MADD.summary: Append new samples to one or more time series

TS.MGET.summary: Get the sample with the highest timestamp from each time series matching a specific filter

TS.MRANGE.args: fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST] [FILTER_BY_TS timestamp [timestamp ...] [FILTER_BY_TS timestamp [timestamp ...] ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max] [WITHLABELS | SELECTED_LABELS label [label ...]] [COUNT count] [[ALIGN alignment] AGGREGATION aggregators bucketDuration [mid | start | end | + | - | ~] [EMPTY]] FILTER filterExpr [filterExpr ...] [GROUPBY label REDUCE avg | sum | min | max | range | count | first | last | std.p | std.s | var.p | var.s | twa | countnan | countall]
TS.MRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series by filters in forward direction

TS.MREVRANGE.args: fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST] [FILTER_BY_TS timestamp [timestamp ...] [FILTER_BY_TS timestamp [timestamp ...] ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max] [WITHLABELS | SELECTED_LABELS label [label ...]] [COUNT count] [[ALIGN alignment] AGGREGATION aggregators bucketDuration [mid | start | end | + | - | ~] [EMPTY]] FILTER filterExpr [filterExpr ...] [GROUPBY label REDUCE avg | sum | min | max | range | count | first | last | std.p | std.s | var.p | var.s | twa | countnan | countall]
TS.MREVRANGE.summary: Query a range across multiple time series by filters in reverse direction

TS.QUERYINDEX.summary: Get all time series keys matching a filter list

TS.RANGE.args: key fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST] [FILTER_BY_TS ts [ts ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max] [COUNT count] [[ALIGN align] AGGREGATION aggregators bucketDuration [BUCKETTIMESTAMP start | - | end | + | mid | ~] [EMPTY]]
TS.RANGE.summary: Query a range in forward direction

TS.REVRANGE.args: key fromTimestamp toTimestamp [LATEST] [FILTER_BY_TS ts [ts ...]] [FILTER_BY_VALUE min max] [COUNT count] [[ALIGN align] AGGREGATION aggregators bucketDuration [BUCKETTIMESTAMP start | - | end | + | mid | ~] [EMPTY]]
TS.REVRANGE.summary: Query a range in reverse direction

TTL.summary: Returns the expiration time in seconds of a key.

TYPE.summary: Determines the type of value stored at a key.

UNLINK.summary: Asynchronously deletes one or more keys.

UNSUBSCRIBE.summary: Stops listening to messages posted to channels.

UNWATCH.summary: Forgets about watched keys of a transaction.

VADD.args: key [dim] FP32 | VALUES vector element [CAS] [NOQUANT | BIN | Q8] [EF build-exploration-factor] [SETATTR attributes] [M numlinks]
VADD.summary: Add one or more elements to a vector set, or update its vector if it already exists

VCARD.summary: Return the number of elements in a vector set

VDIM.summary: Return the dimension of vectors in the vector set

VEMB.summary: Return the vector associated with an element

VGETATTR.summary: Retrieve the JSON attributes of elements

VINFO.summary: Return information about a vector set

VISMEMBER.summary: Check if an element exists in a vector set

VLINKS.summary: Return the neighbors of an element at each layer in the HNSW graph

VRANDMEMBER.summary: Return one or multiple random members from a vector set

VRANGE.summary: Return vector set elements in a lex range

VREM.summary: Remove an element from a vector set

VSETATTR.summary: Associate or remove the JSON attributes of elements

VSIM.args: key ELE | FP32 | VALUES vector_or_element [WITHSCORES] [WITHATTRIBS] [COUNT count] [EPSILON max_distance] [EF search-exploration-factor] [FILTER expression] [FILTER-EF max-filtering-effort] [TRUTH] [NOTHREAD]

WAIT.summary: Blocks until the asynchronous replication of all preceding write commands sent by the connection is completed.

WAITAOF.summary: Blocks until all of the preceding write commands sent by the connection are written to the append-only file of the master and/or replicas.

WATCH.summary: Monitors changes to keys to determine the execution of a transaction.

XACK.summary: Returns the number of messages that were successfully acknowledged by the consumer group member of a stream.

XACKDEL.args: key group [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED] numids id [id ...]
XACKDEL.summary: Acknowledges and deletes one or multiple messages for a stream consumer group.

XADD.args: key [NOMKSTREAM] [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED] [IDMPAUTO pid | pid iid] [MAXLEN | MINID [= | ~] threshold [LIMIT count]] * | id field value [field value ...]
XADD.summary: Appends a new message to a stream. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

XAUTOCLAIM.args: key group consumer min-idle-time start [COUNT count] [JUSTID]
XAUTOCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of messages in a consumer group, as if the messages were delivered to as consumer group member.

XCFGSET.summary: Sets the IDMP configuration parameters for a stream.

XCLAIM.args: key group consumer min-idle-time id [id ...] [IDLE ms] [TIME unix-time-milliseconds] [RETRYCOUNT count] [FORCE] [JUSTID] [LASTID lastid]
XCLAIM.summary: Changes, or acquires, ownership of a message in a consumer group, as if the message was delivered a consumer group member.

XDEL.summary: Returns the number of messages after removing them from a stream.

XDELEX.summary: Deletes one or multiple entries from the stream.

XGROUP.summary: A container for consumer groups commands.
XGROUP.args: CREATE | CREATECONSUMER | DELCONSUMER | DESTROY | HELP | SETID

XGROUP CREATECONSUMER.summary: Creates a consumer in a consumer group.

XGROUP DELCONSUMER.summary: Deletes a consumer from a consumer group.

XGROUP HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

XGROUP SETID.summary: Sets the last-delivered ID of a consumer group.

XIDMPRECORD.summary: An internal command for setting IDMP metadata on an existing stream message.

XINFO.summary: A container for stream introspection commands.

XINFO CONSUMERS.summary: Returns a list of the consumers in a consumer group.

XINFO GROUPS.summary: Returns a list of the consumer groups of a stream.

XINFO HELP.summary: Returns helpful text about the different subcommands.

XLEN.summary: Return the number of messages in a stream.

XNACK.args: key group SILENT | FAIL | FATAL numids id [id ...] [RETRYCOUNT count] [FORCE]
XNACK.summary: Releases claimed messages back to the group's PEL without acknowledging them, making them available for re-delivery.

XPENDING.args: key group [[IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]]
XPENDING.summary: Returns the information and entries from a stream consumer group's pending entries list.

XRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs.

XREAD.summary: Returns messages from multiple streams with IDs greater than the ones requested. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

XREADGROUP.args: group consumer [COUNT count] [BLOCK milliseconds] [CLAIM min-idle-time] [NOACK] key [key ...] id [id ...]
XREADGROUP.summary: Returns new or historical messages from a stream for a consumer in a group. Blocks until a message is available otherwise.

XREVRANGE.summary: Returns the messages from a stream within a range of IDs in reverse order.

XSETID.summary: An internal command for replicating stream values.

XTRIM.args: key MAXLEN | MINID [= | ~] threshold [LIMIT count] [KEEPREF | DELREF | ACKED]
XTRIM.summary: Deletes messages from the beginning of a stream.

ZADD.args: key [NX | XX] [GT | LT] [CH] [INCR] score member [score member ...]
ZADD.summary: Adds one or more members to a sorted set, or updates their scores. Creates the key if it doesn't exist.

ZCARD.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set.

ZCOUNT.summary: Returns the count of members in a sorted set that have scores within a range.

ZDIFF.summary: Returns the difference between multiple sorted sets.

ZDIFFSTORE.summary: Stores the difference of multiple sorted sets in a key.

ZINCRBY.summary: Increments the score of a member in a sorted set.

ZINTER.args: numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [WEIGHTS weight ...]] [SUM | MIN | MAX | COUNT] [WITHSCORES]
ZINTER.summary: Returns the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

ZINTERCARD.summary: Returns the number of members of the intersect of multiple sorted sets.

ZINTERSTORE.args: destination numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [WEIGHTS weight ...]] [SUM | MIN | MAX | COUNT]
ZINTERSTORE.summary: Stores the intersect of multiple sorted sets in a key.

ZLEXCOUNT.summary: Returns the number of members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

ZMPOP.summary: Returns the highest- or lowest-scoring members from one or more sorted sets after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZMSCORE.summary: Returns the score of one or more members in a sorted set.

ZPOPMAX.summary: Returns the highest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZPOPMIN.summary: Returns the lowest-scoring members from a sorted set after removing them. Deletes the sorted set if the last member was popped.

ZRANDMEMBER.summary: Returns one or more random members from a sorted set.

ZRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes.

ZRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range.

ZRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores.

ZRANGESTORE.args: dst src min max [BYSCORE | BYLEX] [REV] [offset count]
ZRANGESTORE.summary: Stores a range of members from sorted set in a key.

ZRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by ascending scores.

ZREM.summary: Removes one or more members from a sorted set. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYLEX.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYRANK.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of indexes. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREMRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Removes members in a sorted set within a range of scores. Deletes the sorted set if all members were removed.

ZREVRANGE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.

ZREVRANGEBYLEX.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a lexicographical range in reverse order.

ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.summary: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of scores in reverse order.

ZREVRANK.summary: Returns the index of a member in a sorted set ordered by descending scores.

ZSCAN.summary: Iterates over members and scores of a sorted set.

ZSCORE.summary: Returns the score of a member in a sorted set.

ZUNION.args: numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [WEIGHTS weight ...]] [SUM | MIN | MAX | COUNT] [WITHSCORES]
ZUNION.summary: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.

ZUNIONSTORE.args: destination numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [WEIGHTS weight ...]] [SUM | MIN | MAX | COUNT]
ZUNIONSTORE.summary: Stores the union of multiple sorted sets in a key.

_FT.CONFIG HELP.summary: Help description of runtime configuration options
